section.title: Overview
section.para: The rust_http_webhook module provides fire-and-forget &http; POST webhook delivery for &sip; event payloads. Script functions return immediately while delivery happens asynchronously in a background tokio task within each worker process.

This module is useful for pushing &sip; event data to external systems (CDR collectors, fraud detection, real-time analytics) without blocking &sip; message processing. If the internal queue is full, payloads are silently dropped and a counter is incremented.

Features include custom &http; headers for authentication or metadata, fanout delivery to multiple &url;s, retry with exponential backoff, message batching for high-volume deployments, &sip; method filtering, and &http; error logging with per-status-code statistics.

section.title: How it works
section.para: Each &osips; worker process maintains its own independent fire-and-forget &http; dispatcher backed by a tokio runtime. When webhook() is called from the script, the payload string is enqueued into a bounded channel. A background task dequeues payloads and sends them as &http; POST requests to the configured &url;(s).
If the queue is full (exceeds max_queue), the payload is dropped and the internal drop counter is incremented. The script function always returns 1 (success) unless the dispatcher is not initialized.
When multiple &url;s are configured (comma-separated), the payload is sent to ALL &url;s (fanout). Each &url; is handled independently — a failure on one does not affect the others.

When max_retries is greater than 0, failed &http; requests (non-2xx responses or network errors) are retried with exponential backoff. The delay doubles after each attempt: retry_delay_ms * 2^(attempt-1).
Custom headers can be added to every POST request using the headers parameter. This is useful for authentication tokens, source identification, or any custom metadata.
The &http; client respects the configured timeout and content type. For HTTPS endpoints, a custom CA certificate file can be specified via tls_ca_file.

section.title: Message Batching
section.para: When batch_size is set to a value greater than 1, the module accumulates multiple payloads and sends them as a single &http; POST containing a JSON array. This reduces &http; overhead for high-volume deployments.
The batch is flushed (sent) when either condition is met:

listitem.para: The number of accumulated messages reaches batch_size.
The time since the first message in the current batch exceeds batch_timeout_ms milliseconds.

section.para: If individual payloads are valid JSON values (objects, arrays, numbers, strings), they are included as-is in the array. If a payload is not valid JSON, it is wrapped as a JSON string. For example, three JSON object payloads become: [{"method":"INVITE"},{"method":"BYE"},{"method":"ACK"}].
When batch_size is 1 (the default), batching is disabled and each payload is sent individually, preserving backward compatibility.

section.title: Method Filtering
section.para: The method_filter parameter restricts webhook delivery to specific &sip; methods. When set, only messages whose request method matches one of the listed values trigger a webhook. All other messages are silently skipped (the function returns 1, not an error).
Method names are case-insensitive. If the parameter is not set or is empty, all &sip; methods trigger webhooks (default behavior).

section.title: Error Logging and Statistics
section.para: When log_errors is set to 1, each &http; failure (non-2xx response or timeout) is logged at WARN level with the target &url; and status code. This is useful for diagnosing connectivity issues with webhook endpoints.

The module tracks per-category error counters accessible via webhook_stats(): errors_4xx (client errors), errors_5xx (server errors), and errors_timeout (timeouts/network errors). These counters are always maintained regardless of the log_errors setting.

section.title: &osips; Modules
section.para:
The following modules must be loaded before this module:
None.

section.title: External Libraries or Applications
section.para:
The following libraries or applications must be installed before running &osips; with this module loaded:
None. All &http; functionality is compiled into the Rust shared library.

section.title: url (string)
section.para: The HTTP(S) endpoint &url;(s) where webhook payloads are POSTed. This parameter is required. The module will fail to load if this parameter is not set or is empty.
Multiple &url;s can be specified as a comma-separated list for fanout delivery. Each payload is sent to ALL &url;s.

para.emphasis: Default value is None (must be set).

section.title: max_queue (integer)
section.para: Maximum number of payloads that can be queued per worker process before new payloads are dropped. Each worker maintains its own independent queue. Must be at least 1; values below 1 are clamped to 1 at module load.

section.title: http_timeout (integer)
section.para: &http; request timeout in seconds. Requests that exceed this timeout are abandoned. Must be at least 1; values below 1 are clamped to 1 at module load.

section.title: content_type (string)
section.para: The Content-Type header value sent with each POST request.

section.title: tls_ca_file (string)
section.para: Path to a custom CA certificate file (PEM format) for TLS verification of the webhook endpoint. If not set, the system default CA bundle is used.

section.title: headers (string)
section.para: Custom &http; headers to include in every POST request. Multiple headers are separated by the pipe character (|). Each header is specified as Name: Value. Whitespace around names and values is trimmed. Entries without a colon are silently ignored.
This is useful for authentication tokens, API keys, or source identification headers.

para.emphasis: Default value is None (no custom headers).

section.title: max_retries (integer)
section.para: Maximum number of retry attempts on &http; failure (non-2xx response or network error). Set to 0 to disable retries. Retries use exponential backoff with the base delay set by retry_delay_ms.

section.title: retry_delay_ms (integer)
section.para: Base retry delay in milliseconds. The actual delay uses exponential backoff: retry_delay_ms * 2^(attempt-1). For example, with retry_delay_ms=1000 and max_retries=3, the delays are 1000ms, 2000ms, and 4000ms.

section.title: batch_size (integer)
section.para: Number of payloads to accumulate before sending them as a single &http; POST containing a JSON array. Set to 1 to disable batching (each payload is sent individually).

When batching is enabled (value greater than 1), payloads are buffered and flushed either when the batch reaches this size or when batch_timeout_ms milliseconds elapse, whichever comes first. The POST body is a JSON array containing all accumulated payloads.

section.title: batch_timeout_ms (integer)
section.para: Maximum time in milliseconds to wait before flushing a partial batch. If the batch has not reached batch_size within this interval, it is sent anyway. This prevents messages from being held indefinitely during low-traffic periods.
Only effective when batch_size is greater than 1.

section.title: method_filter (string)
section.para: Comma-separated list of &sip; methods for which webhooks should be sent. If not set or empty, webhooks are sent for all &sip; methods (default behavior).

When set, only messages whose request method matches one of the listed methods will trigger a webhook. Other messages are silently skipped — the webhook() function returns 1 (not an error) and the filtered counter is incremented.
Method names are case-insensitive.

section.title: log_errors (integer)
section.para: Enable logging of &http; delivery failures. When set to 1, each failed webhook delivery (non-2xx response or timeout/network error) is logged at WARN level with the target &url; and &http; status code.
Error statistics (errors_4xx, errors_5xx, errors_timeout) are always tracked regardless of this setting.
Enqueue a payload string for asynchronous &http; POST delivery to the configured webhook &url;(s). The function returns immediately without waiting for the &http; request to complete.
If multiple &url;s are configured, the payload is sent to all of them (fanout). If the per-worker queue is full, the payload is silently dropped and a warning is logged.

If method_filter is configured and the current &sip; request method is not in the filter set, the payload is silently skipped (not enqueued). The function returns 1 and the filtered counter is incremented.
If batch_size is greater than 1, the payload is added to the current batch buffer. The batch is sent when it reaches the configured size or the timeout expires.
Meaning of the parameters is as follows:

listitem.para: payload (string) - The payload body to POST. Typically a JSON string constructed using pseudo-variables. This parameter is mandatory.

section.para: Return codes:

listitem.para: 1 - payload enqueued (or dropped due to full queue, or filtered out).

section.para: This function can be used from REQUEST_ROUTE, FAILURE_ROUTE, and ONREPLY_ROUTE.

example.title: webhook with batching and filtering

section.para: Retrieve module statistics as a JSON string. Sets $var(webhook_stats) with a JSON object containing all tracked counters for the current worker process.
The JSON object includes the following fields: sent, dropped, failed, retried, retry_exhausted, errors_4xx, errors_5xx, errors_timeout, and filtered.
Return codes:
This function can be used from REQUEST_ROUTE, FAILURE_ROUTE, and ONREPLY_ROUTE.

section.title: $var(webhook_stats)
section.para: JSON string containing the module statistics for the current worker process. Set by webhook_stats().
Number of webhook payloads successfully enqueued for delivery.
Number of webhook payloads dropped due to a full queue.
Number of webhook payloads that failed to deliver (&http; errors, timeouts) after all retry attempts.
Number of individual retry attempts made across all payloads.
Number of payloads that exhausted all retry attempts without a successful delivery.
Number of &http; 4xx (client error) responses received from webhook endpoints.
Number of &http; 5xx (server error) responses received from webhook endpoints.
Number of webhook deliveries that failed due to a timeout or network error.
